fields.description:
## Why

Per-criterion **blocking is already done** (`code_review/registry.threshold_for` + kernel Pass-3 +
detector fail-closed). What is missing is **attestation integration**: a project criterion's
verdict/coverage must fold into the signed (kind-keyed) attestation, and activating/editing/
disabling a criterion must invalidate a prior attestation — but `claim_gate_check` never
re-compares the routing version, and `registry_version()` hashes only the PACKAGED routing
(`attest.py:44`), so an overlay change produces an identical version and does NOT invalidate.

## What

- `registry_version(repo_root=None)` additionally hashes the `.rebar/` overlay when `repo_root` is
  given; thread `repo_root` through **every** caller: `claim_gate_check` → `compute_validity`, and
  also `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` (which call `registry_version()`
  without it today). So activating/editing/disabling a criterion invalidates a prior attestation at
  claim time.
- A disabled built-in is recorded in a `disabled_builtins: [ids]` field in the verdict `coverage`
  dict (folded into the signed manifest) so the cert reflects reduced coverage; additive/
  expand-contract (absent on old certs → treated as stale → re-review, never a crash).
- Cross-gate: this applies to **any** gate attestation backed by the shared registry — plan-review
  now; code-review once it signs (code-review signs nothing today; the `attest.py` port to
  code-review rides with story `#6`).

## Scope

- Files: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (registry_version(repo_root), coverage field,
  threading), the claim-gate path.
- Depends on story `#1` (the overlay must exist to hash) and story `#2` (DET blocking).
- Blocking itself is NOT in scope (already shipped via `threshold_for` + kernel Pass-3).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `registry_version(repo_root)` hashes the `.rebar/` overlay; threaded through
      `claim_gate_check`/`compute_validity`/`drift_refresh_candidate`/`remediation_mode_candidate`.
- [ ] Activating/editing/disabling a project criterion invalidates a prior plan-review attestation
      at claim time; an unchanged overlay does not churn (regver == today when overlay absent).
- [ ] A disabled built-in appears in `coverage.disabled_builtins` in the signed manifest.
- [ ] Expand-contract: an old attestation lacking the new basis is treated as stale (re-review),
      never a crash.
- [ ] A project criterion's verdict + coverage fold into the signed attestation; it can never
      rebind a built-in.


## compute_validity regver check + disabled_builtins manifest line (remediation, run 1)

### `compute_validity` gains a regver staleness check (clears G6 blocker 1)
Threading `repo_root` into `compute_validity` is necessary but NOT sufficient — its plan-review
branch must ALSO compare the attestation's recorded regver against the current one:
`manifest_regver(manifest) != registry_version(repo_root)` → the record reads as **not valid**
with reason `"stale-regver"` (a new invalidation reason alongside the existing
material/head-sha/reopen checks). So activating/editing/disabling a project criterion (which
changes `registry_version(repo_root)` because it now hashes the `.rebar/` overlay) makes a prior
attestation read stale at claim time. `manifest_regver(manifest)` is a small helper that extracts
the `regver:` line from the signed manifest (mirroring the existing `manifest_material`). An
attestation with NO regver line (pre-this-change) → treated as stale → re-review (expand-contract,
never a crash).

### `disabled_builtins` is a dedicated signed manifest line (clears G6 blocker 2)
`build_manifest()` emits a NEW `disabled_builtins: [id,id,...]` line (sorted, deterministic) WHEN
the verdict `coverage` carries disabled built-ins (from the overlay disabling a built-in via ef7e's
merge) — so the cert reflects reduced coverage rather than silent omission. A `manifest_disabled_builtins(manifest)`
parser reads it back (like `manifest_material`). The line is ADDITIVE/expand-contract: absent on old
certs (parsed as `[]`), present only when non-empty, so a clean run's manifest is byte-identical to
today (attestation-safe). `verify_signature` re-derives the same manifest and checks the HMAC as usual.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions)

- [ ] `compute_validity` (plan-review branch) adds a `manifest_regver != registry_version(repo_root)`
      staleness check returning reason `stale-regver`; a missing regver line → stale (re-review).
      A test proves activating/editing a project criterion invalidates a prior attestation.
- [ ] `build_manifest` emits a deterministic `disabled_builtins: [ids]` line when the verdict
      coverage has disabled built-ins; a `manifest_disabled_builtins` parser reads it; absent/empty
      on a clean run (byte-identical manifest). A test asserts the round-trip + HMAC re-verify.
